title: The halo and plug depth
wikitext:
[]: The halo, and plug depth             Quote Post by wjr » Sun Jan 09, 2011 5:32 am Hi everyone, I just saw Rebuild HD on youtube, and couldn't help thinking about the halo. So far my opinion is the halo is associated with the plug-depth: in rare instances, when the pilot is achieving a certain insane amount of synch rate with the eva unit, the plug dives further into the core - not in a physical sense, as it would become an eva-chestburster, but in a metaphysical way, sort of like electromagnetic pulsation. Then it is possible a mini black-hole be created directly behind in the same direction where the plug is diving - which happens to be above the head, creating a god-like appearance. This actually reminds of a jet exhaust fume, created behind an ultra-powerful engine. And for a moment it looks like there is another eva unit that also possesses the halo, and the pilot descends from the moon. I guess the pilot also has an unbelievable reading of synch rate. BTW, this is what I consider Evangelion at its best - mixing science-fiction with quashi-religious metaphor. No other series come even close to Evangelion in this creative literature department. What do you think?

[]:             Quote Post by Jornophelanthas » Sun Jan 09, 2011 8:09 am Angels also tend to show haloes (or at least the Fourth Angel in 1.0), mostly while they are activating some kind of high energy power. Also, I can recommend watching the first Neon Genesis Evangelion series, because it will tell you something about Kaworu Nagisa (Unit-06's pilot) and his synchronisation rate. (He only appears very near to the end.) Note that the reason for it could well be different in Rebuild. Also note that the NGE series placed heavy importance on the concept of "synchronisation rate", while Rebuild only mentions it once or twice, while "plug depth" is entirely specific to Rebuild. What I can say about plug depth, is that it does appear to indicate physical proximity of the entry plug to the Evangelion's core. At multiple times during Rebuild 2.0, it is suggested what happens when plug depth gets too high: the pilot starts to lose his/her human shape. Basically, the pilot's physical body starts to dissolve in the entry plug's LCL, which I suspect is also the blood-like liquid that fills the Angels' cores and the red oceans. - Asuka's plug in Unit-03 is drawn in far too deep by the Angel. We do not know the effects of this yet. I do believe that the moment Asuka starts floating inside her entry plug as her controls disappear, shows us that she is losing her physical shape, and her soul is drawn into the Angel. - Mari's plug in Unit-02 is drawn in to a dangerous level by Beast mode. We see her eyes start glowing green, and her face is morphed to match Unit-02's bestial expression (wide mouth, big teeth, glasses losing their original shape). I believe this is not an animation quirk, but actual fluidity of her human shape. Mari is able to recover afterwards, though. - Rei, after being absorbed into the 10th Angel's core is shown as a naked, luminescent entity. I believe this is her soul without her physical body, as her body dissolved inside Unit-00's core as it was assimilated into the Angel's core. - As Shinji goes berserk inside Unit-01, his eyes glow red. As his plug depth rises to the highest level seen so far, way beyond safe levels (once he starts to claw his way through the Angel's core towards Rei), he becomes a similarly naked luminescent white body as Rei. This indicates that he lost his physical shape (probably without realizing it, and certainly without caring about it) and is purely a soul reaching out toward Rei's. As he saves her, he pulls her into Unit-01's core. It remains to be seen if (and in how much time) Shinji and Rei will be able to reshape their physical bodies inside Unit-01's entry plug. - Kaworu's plug depth in Unit-06 appears to remain within normal levels, as he is not seen to "liquify" or dissolve into a luminescent white soul, even though the Evangelion shows a halo. However, Kaworu is far from your typical human (He can survive in the moon's atmosphere!). Add to this that we know from Gendo's and Fuyutsuki's trip to the moon that Unit-06 is constructed far differently from the rest of the Evangelion series. It is a "true god" rather than a "false god". What this means is subject for speculation. (Personally, I believe that Unit-06 is Lilith put into an Evangelion suit, while the "Lilith" beneath Tokyo-3 is actually something else.) And yes, I believe it is no coincidence that all the main pilots are shown losing their human shape at some point during Rebuild 2.0. (I don't count Kaworu yet, because he has barely had any screentime so far. Also, it remains to be seen how human he even is.)

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sun Jan 09, 2011 9:29 am I think the Halo is connected tho the Eva's "Godly/angel-like" aspects - They are created from Angels. Shinji unlocked his Eva's God-like Powers, and Kaworu can probably use those of EVA 06 as easily as he can move his own Hands. (If certain things from the old series carry over to Rebuild - which seems likely since he isn't even wearing an interface Headset.) What could be connected to plug depht are the glowing eyes. (Shinji's glow red when he goes God!Mode, Mari's glow green when she reaches a "negative plug depht" and Asuka's glow blue for a Moment when she is "pulled inside". The 'glowing eyes collor' either depends on the EVA or the Pilot - I don't think a certain color is accossiated with a certain Depht, since Shinji's stay red all the time while his plug Depht comes gradually closer and closer to the far end of the scale.

[]:             Quote Post by SaltyJoe » Sun Jan 09, 2011 11:09 am Jornophelanthas wrote: Basically, the pilot's physical body starts to dissolve in the entry plug's LCL, which I suspect is also the blood-like liquid that fills the Angels' cores and the red oceans.

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Sun Jan 09, 2011 2:02 pm SaltyJoe wrote: It is pretty peculiar that out of all the major characters, Shinji is the only one who doesn't appear in the trailer for 3.0.

[]:             Quote Post by Jornophelanthas » Sun Jan 09, 2011 2:10 pm Azathoth wrote: Sure he does, he's the purple one with the spear stuck through his ass.

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Sun Jan 09, 2011 2:19 pm Jornophelanthas wrote: Are you confusing Shinji with Unit-01, or are you saying Shinji (and Rei) became one with Unit-01 and actually is the Evangelion?

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sun Jan 09, 2011 3:00 pm Azathoth wrote: I believe that's Shinji - or at least he reaches The Great Beyond-depth, which seems to be much the same thing.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva 04 » Sun Jan 09, 2011 4:22 pm Jornophelanthas wrote: Angels also tend to show haloes (or at least the Fourth Angel in 1.0), mostly while they are activating some kind of high energy power. Also, I can recommend watching the first Neon Genesis Evangelion series, because it will tell you something about Kaworu Nagisa (Unit-06's pilot) and his synchronisation rate. (He only appears very near to the end.) Note that the reason for it could well be different in Rebuild. Also note that the NGE series placed heavy importance on the concept of "synchronisation rate", while Rebuild only mentions it once or twice, while "plug depth" is entirely specific to Rebuild. What I can say about plug depth, is that it does appear to indicate physical proximity of the entry plug to the Evangelion's core. At multiple times during Rebuild 2.0, it is suggested what happens when plug depth gets too high: the pilot starts to lose his/her human shape. Basically, the pilot's physical body starts to dissolve in the entry plug's LCL, which I suspect is also the blood-like liquid that fills the Angels' cores and the red oceans. - Asuka's plug in Unit-03 is drawn in far too deep by the Angel. We do not know the effects of this yet. I do believe that the moment Asuka starts floating inside her entry plug as her controls disappear, shows us that she is losing her physical shape, and her soul is drawn into the Angel. - Mari's plug in Unit-02 is drawn in to a dangerous level by Beast mode. We see her eyes start glowing green, and her face is morphed to match Unit-02's bestial expression (wide mouth, big teeth, glasses losing their original shape). I believe this is not an animation quirk, but actual fluidity of her human shape. Mari is able to recover afterwards, though. - Rei, after being absorbed into the 10th Angel's core is shown as a naked, luminescent entity. I believe this is her soul without her physical body, as her body dissolved inside Unit-00's core as it was assimilated into the Angel's core. - As Shinji goes berserk inside Unit-01, his eyes glow red. As his plug depth rises to the highest level seen so far, way beyond safe levels (once he starts to claw his way through the Angel's core towards Rei), he becomes a similarly naked luminescent white body as Rei. This indicates that he lost his physical shape (probably without realizing it, and certainly without caring about it) and is purely a soul reaching out toward Rei's. As he saves her, he pulls her into Unit-01's core. It remains to be seen if (and in how much time) Shinji and Rei will be able to reshape their physical bodies inside Unit-01's entry plug. - Kaworu's plug depth in Unit-06 appears to remain within normal levels, as he is not seen to "liquify" or dissolve into a luminescent white soul, even though the Evangelion shows a halo. However, Kaworu is far from your typical human (He can survive in the moon's atmosphere!). Add to this that we know from Gendo's and Fuyutsuki's trip to the moon that Unit-06 is constructed far differently from the rest of the Evangelion series. It is a "true god" rather than a "false god". What this means is subject for speculation. (Personally, I believe that Unit-06 is Lilith put into an Evangelion suit, while the "Lilith" beneath Tokyo-3 is actually something else.) And yes, I believe it is no coincidence that all the main pilots are shown losing their human shape at some point during Rebuild 2.0. (I don't count Kaworu yet, because he has barely had any screentime so far. Also, it remains to be seen how human he even is.)

[]:             Quote Post by Jornophelanthas » Sun Jan 09, 2011 4:36 pm Eva 04 wrote: The fourth angel wasnt the only one shown to have a Halo. Sachiel had one and so did Ramiel. And in 2.0,the 8th angel had one as well. So did the 3rd angel. The only angels that didnt have one would be the 7th,9th and 10th. Just thought I'd point this out.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva 04 » Sun Jan 09, 2011 4:53 pm Jornophelanthas wrote: Thank you. I didn't have this information with me as I wrote the post. (Also note that "Sachiel" is the Fourth Angel in Rebuild. Since none of the Rebuild Angels are named, I am using quotes to indicate they are not officially named this way.) I should repeat, though, that the theme of the haloes in Rebuild is something I do not fully understand. I mean, I get the allusion to Christian divinity, but not its meaning within the story, or its explanation in-universe (if there is ever going to be one). --- @Kendrix: Mari does not liquify, but her features do lose some humanity/consistency while Unit-02 is in beast mode. (No normal human being in Evangelion has a mouth that wide.) Since this is described as "negative plug depth" by the tech crew, I am assuming tihs is the opposite of Shinji's "plug depth ratings exceeding" [i.e. too high in the positives] "safe values by far". (loose paraphrases) Also, I believe the term "negative plug depth" was also used to refer to Asuka inside Unit-03. I need to look this up, though.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Sun Jan 09, 2011 5:00 pm Bardiel (9th) has a halo right before jumping towards Eva-01.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sun Jan 09, 2011 5:02 pm Jornophelanthas wrote: @Kendrix: Mari does not liquify, but her features do lose some humanity/consistency while Unit-02 is in beast mode. (No normal human being in Evangelion has a mouth that wide.) .

[]:             Quote Post by Jornophelanthas » Sun Jan 09, 2011 6:10 pm Kendrix wrote: When was that? Screenshot plz. i don't recall anything like that, but I might've missed it.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva 04 » Sun Jan 09, 2011 7:18 pm Ornette wrote: Bardiel (9th) has a halo right before jumping towards Eva-01.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Sun Jan 09, 2011 7:29 pm

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Sun Jan 09, 2011 7:32 pm To some extent, there's a correlation between the halo and flight: Sachiel and Bardiel only manifest theirs when levitating (possibly this goes for Tunnel also? can't recall), and of course Kaworu's magical flying Eva has one...and not long after Unit 01 picks up its halo, it starts floating too.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva 04 » Sun Jan 09, 2011 7:41 pm Azathoth wrote: To some extent, there's a correlation between the halo and flight: Sachiel and Bardiel only manifest theirs when levitating (possibly this goes for Tunnel also? can't recall), and of course Kaworu's magical flying Eva has one...and not long after Unit 01 picks up its halo, it starts floating too.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Jan 09, 2011 9:38 pm Jorno wrote: "plug depth" is entirely specific to Rebuild.

[]:             Quote Post by Assasinate » Sun Jan 16, 2011 8:49 pm yeah i noticed about that, but when sachiel levitates, i didnt seen any halo in him, what ive seen to him is some kind of an aura explosion and then he floats already...
